context: Tests whether the agent uses the correct ElevenLabs JavaScript SDK package, proper imports, correct API method for sound effects, proper stream handling, environment-based authentication, and descriptive prompts.

checklist.name: Correct npm package
checklist.description: package.json lists @elevenlabs/elevenlabs-js as a dependency (not the deprecated 'elevenlabs' package)

checklist.name: No deprecated package
checklist.description: Neither package.json nor any JS file references the deprecated 'elevenlabs' npm package (v1.x) as a dependency or import

checklist.name: Correct API method
checklist.description: Uses client.textToSoundEffects.convert() (camelCase) to generate sound effects

checklist.name: Text parameter provided
checklist.description: Passes a 'text' property in the convert() call for each sound effect

checklist.name: Stream handling
checklist.description: Uses .pipe() with a write stream (e.g., createWriteStream) or equivalent streaming approach to save the audio response

checklist.name: Environment variable auth
checklist.description: Client is instantiated without hardcoding an API key -- relies on ELEVENLABS_API_KEY environment variable or reads from process.env

checklist.name: Descriptive prompts
checklist.description: The sample manifest prompts are specific and descriptive (more than one or two generic words per prompt, e.g., NOT just 'jump' but something like 'bouncy spring jump sound effect')

checklist.name: MP3 output extension
checklist.description: Output filenames in the manifest use .mp3 extension (the default format)

checklist.name: Await on convert
checklist.description: The convert() call is properly awaited (uses await keyword or .then() pattern)

checklist.name: Multiple sound effects
checklist.description: The manifest contains at least 3 distinct sound effect entries
